arch_note: arch=b64 and arch=b32 are DISTINCT filters (libaudit.c:1419-1422). A 32-bit process issues 32-bit syscalls matched ONLY by arch=b32, so the paired rules cover both ABIs. They are NOT a duplicate: deduping loses 32-bit coverage; the cost is the SUM of the two arch slices (b64 dominant, b32 a small-but-nonzero slice). f3 section 2.4.

rules.note: 64-bit ABI execve, narrowed by auid>=1000 (MEDIUM). The bulk of process starts on a modern host.
32-bit ABI execve, SAME key. Distinct arch filter, NOT a duplicate. Small but nonzero slice (few 32-bit processes on a modern host). Deduping it loses 32-bit coverage.

dominant_tier: MEDIUM (additive, b64) + LOW (b32); SUM of two arch slices
rate_band_basis: f3 section 6: narrowed execve MEDIUM (b64 bulk); b32 a small real slice. Total = sum of both arches; they are NOT redundant.
